zh.lifeSummary: 當Agnes le Boteler於1272年出生在英國越可爾普倫普頓 在 斯波福斯時，她的父親Nigel le Boteler19歲，母親Agnes Boteler21歲。她於1297年與Sir William de Middleton在英國越可爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1311年在她的家鄉享年39歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Agnes le Boteler于1272年出生在英国越可爾普倫普頓 在 斯波福斯时，她的父亲Nigel le Boteler19岁，母亲Agnes Boteler21岁。她于1297年与Sir William de Middleton在英国越可爾结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1311年在她的家乡享年39岁。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Boteler
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a maker of leather bottles, from an agent derivative of Middle English botel, Old French bouteille ‘bottle’.

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Agnes le Boteler was born in 1272, in Plumpton in Spofforth, Yorkshire, England, her father, Nigel le Boteler, was 19 and her mother, Agnes Boteler, was 21. She married Sir William de Middleton in 1297, in Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1311, in her hometown, at the age of 39.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Agnes le Boteler est née en 1272, à Plumpton in Spofforth, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Nigel le Boteler, avait 19 ans et sa mère, Agnes Boteler, avait 21 ans. Elle a épousé Sir William de Middleton en 1297, à Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1311, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 39 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Agnes le Boteler 1272 in Plumpton in Spofforth, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Nigel le Boteler 19 und ihre Mutter Agnes Boteler 21. Sie hat 1297 in Yorkshire, England Sir William de Middleton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1311 im Alter von 39 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes le Boteler è nata il nel 1272 nel Plumpton in Spofforth, Yorkshire, England, sua padre, Nigel le Boteler, aveva 19 anni e sua madre, Agnes Boteler, aveva 21 anni. Lei sposato Sir William de Middleton nel 1297, nel Yorkshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1311, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 39 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Agnes le Botelerが1272年にイギリスヨークシュアイアプルンプトン イン スプオフォースで生まれた時，父Nigel le Botelerは19歳，母Agnes Botelerは21歳であった。1297年にイギリスヨークシュアイアでSir William de Middletonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1311年に故郷で39歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes le Boteler(이)가 1272년 영국 요르크실르 플럼프턴 인 스포포스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Nigel le Boteler(은)는 19세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Agnes Boteler(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 1297년 영국 요르크실르에서 Sir William de Middleton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1311년 그녀의 고향에서 39세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes le Boteler nasceu em 1272, em Plumpton in Spofforth, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Nigel le Boteler, tinha 19 anos e sua mãe, Agnes Boteler, tinha 21 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir William de Middleton em 1297, em Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1311, em sua cidade natal, com 39 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Agnes le Boteler родилась в 1272 г. в местности плумптон ин споффорт, еркшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Nigel le Boteler было 19 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnes Boteler, 21. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir William de Middleton в 1297 г. в местности еркшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1311 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 39 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Agnes le Boteler nació en 1272, en Plumpton in Spofforth, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Nigel le Boteler, tenía 19 años y su mamá, Agnes Boteler, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Sir William de Middleton en 1297, en Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1311, en su pueblo, a la edad de 39 años.
